image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: 128 new Saturn moons discovered: Scientists say they are fragments of ancient worlds
item.description: Saturn now holds the record for the most known moons after 128 new discoveries. These small irregular moons offer clues about Saturn's violent past and ancient collisions. Astronomers used the Canada-France-Hawaii Telescope to detect these faint objects over several years. The findings suggest a major collision occurred within Saturn's moon system recently. This event likely created many of Saturn's numerous small irregular moons.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Indian-origin woman held in Canada for $60,000 rental scam
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: From 200 to 1,500: Hawaii's Aeʻo bird recovery is a conservation success story
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: A Ford worker says he was wrongly fired over a $1.95 packet of cookies from a self-checkout kiosk, then offered $33,000 in back wages after showing proof of payment
item.description: A Ford employee faced termination after an alleged cookie theft from a self-checkout kiosk. Bank records later proved the $1.95 snack had been paid for by the diabetic worker. Ford acknowledged the error and offered the employee back wages and his old job. However, the former electrician declined the offer and is now preparing to sue the automaker. This incident highlights concerns about workplace technology and employee investigation protocols.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists are introducing two truffle-eating marsupials to rebuild this Australian island destroyed by feral cats
item.description: In a groundbreaking conservation initiative, two endangered marsupial species are set to be reintroduced to Shark Bay. Recent research emphasizes the crucial role of fungal food sources in this process. Utilizing DNA analysis, scientists identified suitable fungi for the boodies and woylies, which are vital for spreading spores during their foraging activities. Their return will revitalize essential ecological dynamics that have been missing from Dirk Hartog Island for many years.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: California is weighing state endangered-species protections for southern resident killer whales, a population of just over 70 animals threatened by noise, pollution and falling salmon prey
item.description: California wildlife officials are reviewing a petition for endangered status for southern resident killer whales. These whales face severe threats from declining salmon and increased human activity. The state is gathering public input on potential new protections for the imperiled population. Washington and Oregon have already listed these whales under their state laws. This action aims to strengthen conservation efforts for the critically low whale numbers.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Can spreading crushed volcanic rock on farms help fight climate change and improve soil health?
item.description: Crushed volcanic rock spread on farms accelerates natural carbon capture processes. This method converts atmospheric carbon dioxide into stable bicarbonate ions for storage. Basalt also releases essential nutrients, improving soil health and crop yields. Field experiments show potential for significant carbon removal alongside agricultural benefits. Ongoing research aims to verify effectiveness and integrate farms into carbon removal strategies.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Congo's Ebola outbreak kills nearly 1,000 as cases climb to 2,473
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: After two years of banning bee-harming pesticides, France allows their return, facing backlash from environmentalists
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: An Australian reef disappears beneath the ocean every day, then returns with waterfalls
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Sri Lankan-origin doctor arrested in Canada for possessing child sexual abuse material
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Meet Hedy Lamarr: Hollywood icon whose invention laid the foundation for modern Wi-Fi
item.description: Hedy Lamarr, widely known for her illustrious acting career, also made significant strides in technology by inventing a communication system that used frequency hopping to encrypt radio signals in wartime. Her pioneering work paved the way for the development of contemporary innovations such as Wi-Fi and Bluetooth. Although her achievements were initially overlooked, she has since been recognized as a trailblazer in both the entertainment and tech industries.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Arizona teen helps elderly woman with dementia in 103F heat, officials warn of confusion as a sign of heatstroke
item.description: Royal Cothrun, a fourteen-year-old boy, demonstrated extraordinary courage during a perilous heatwave by rescuing an elderly woman wandering aimlessly. With a swift instinct, he provided shade and facilitated her communication with her son, actions that were potentially lifesaving. The Arizona fire department is set to commend him for his heroic efforts, highlighting the critical risks associated with severe summer heat.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists are planning to install an 80km underwater curtain to stop an Antarctic glavier from melting, here's why
item.description: In an exciting scientific endeavor, researchers are exploring the idea of constructing a massive underwater curtain to defend Thwaites Glacier against warm ocean currents. This strategic barrier is intended to redirect these warmer waters, aiming to curb the glacier's swift melting. A recent expedition gathered vital information from the Amundsen Sea to determine the project's feasibility, while additional studies in Svalbard focus on understanding the environmental ramifications of the barrier.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: The secret lives of whale sharks finally unveiled!
item.description: New findings have shown that whale sharks are capable of diving deeper than seventy meters when hunting for food. These majestic giants use a variety of feeding strategies, moving beyond just grazing at the surface. Remarkable underwater footage captured via cameras attached to the sharks highlights their feeding dynamics, showcasing low-energy grazing alongside energetic krill chases. This study enhances our grasp of their intricate foraging patterns.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Rampage Jackson vs Sweatergxd controversy explained after MMA legend punches Kick streamer live
item.description: A party encounter between MMA legend Rampage Jackson and Kick streamer Sweatergxd turned physical after the streamer repeatedly touched Jackson inappropriately, including his groin area. After issuing several verbal warnings and telling him not to touch him again, Jackson threw a punch before leaving the scene, with the incident now circulating widely online.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: A Michelin-starred restaurant in South Korea served 49,000 ants as dessert garnish, owner faces jail
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: What happened to Charleston White? YouTuber shot at in Houston by group
item.description: Charleston White says he was shot at during a visit to Houston's Fifth Ward after a group allegedly confronted him over past remarks and asked him to apologize. In a livestream, the comedian confirmed he was not injured, shared his version of the incident, and said he plans to file a report with the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Shrimp feeding in microgravity: Scientists are teaching shrimp to eat in zero gravity
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Aral Sea collapse and revival: World's greatest environmental disasters
